history.text: The territory was organized as a duchy, an early form of regional political rule.
A kingdom was proclaimed, placing monarchy at the centre of Croatia's state authority.
Croatia in personal union with Hungary tied the territory into a wider political structure.
Croatia joined Habsburg Monarchy, tying the country more closely to that regional or international organisation.
Secession from Austria-Hungary changed control of the territory.
The creation of Yugoslavia tied Croatia into a new South Slavic state.
Socialist Republic of Croatia in Yugoslavia introduced a new form of state rule.
A declaration of independence was issued, making sovereignty the central political claim.

worthSeeing.title: Dubrovnik:
worthSeeing.text: A walled Old Town on the Adriatic, UNESCO listed and a Game of Thrones location.

worthSeeing.title: Plitvice Lakes:
worthSeeing.text: Cascading turquoise lakes and waterfalls in a UNESCO national park.

worthSeeing.title: Dalmatian Islands:
worthSeeing.text: Hvar, Brac and Korcula offer beaches, vineyards and medieval villages.
